evals.prompt: I have a SaaS pricing page with 3 plans (Starter $49/mo, Growth $149/mo, Scale $399/mo). I also have customer reviews. How do I implement schema markup to get rich results in Google?
evals.expected_output: JSON-LD schema implementation for the pricing page including Product schema for each plan and Review/AggregateRating schema, with code examples and validation guidance.
assertions: Provides JSON-LD code examples (not Microdata or RDFa)
Covers Product schema for the pricing plans
Includes Google's eligibility requirements for rich results
Mentions validation with Google's Rich Results Test

evals.prompt: What schema types should a SaaS blog use?
evals.expected_output: List of recommended schema types for a SaaS blog (Article, BlogPosting, FAQPage, HowTo, BreadcrumbList) with guidance on when to use each and implementation notes.
assertions: Recommends Article or BlogPosting schema as primary
Mentions FAQPage schema as high-value for SaaS content
Notes which types are most likely to produce rich results
Provides at least one code snippet example

evals.prompt: Do a full SEO audit of my website
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is a full SEO audit task and redirects to seo-audit skill, while offering to assess the structured data component as part of that audit.
assertions: Redirects to seo-audit skill for the full audit
Positions schema markup as one component of SEO
Does not attempt to run a full audit as a schema-markup skill
